This classification places Pilosella aiboensis among other related species within the subfamily Cichorioideae. intro: Pilosella aiboensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), native to the high-altitude alpine meadows of the Himalayas. Traditionally, Pilosella aiboensis has been used in Ayurvedic medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella aiboensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Pilosella aiboensis is often combined with other herbs to create decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
